modules.title: Direct sum of a vector space, Dual Spaces
topics: Direct sum of a vector space

modules.title: Characteristic values and characteristic polynomials, diagonalizable transformations, annihilating polynomials, Cayley‑Hamilton theorem, invariant subspaces and triangular form, simultaneous triangularization and diagonalization, direct sum decompositions, invariant direct sums, primary decomposition theorem

Adjoint of a linear transformation, Inner product spaces, Eigenvalues and eigenvectors, Diagonalization, Invariant subspaces, Unitary and normal transformations, Spectral Theorem, Jordan canonical form
topics: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a linear transformation

modules.title: Canonical forms, Similarity of linear transformations, Reduction to triangular forms, Nilpotent transformations, Primary decomposition theorem, Jordan blocks and Jordan forms, Invariants of linear transformations
Hermitian, Self adjoint, Unitary and normal linear transformation, Symmetric bilinear forms, Skew symmetric bilinear forms, Group preserving bilinear forms

reference_books: P. Halmos, Finite dimensional vector spaces, Springer, 1974.
Peter D. Lax, Linear algebra, Wiley student edition, 1997.
E.B. Vinberg, A course in algebra, Graduate text in Mathematics, volume 56, AMS, 2003.
M. Thamban Nair and Arindama Singh, Linear algebra, Springer, 2018.

modules.title: Unit 2
topics: Perception of cyber criminals (hackers, insurgents and extremist groups)

modules.title: Unit 3
topics: Concept of Cyber Crime and the IT Act, 2000

modules.title: Unit 4
topics: Status of Electronic Records as Evidence under Indian Evidence Act, 1872 and IT Act, 2000
Proof and Management of Electronic Records
Relevancy, Admissibility and Probative Value of E‑Evidence

reference_books: Principles of Cyber crime, Jonathan Clough Cambridge University Press
John R. Vacca, Computer Forensics: Computer Crime Scene Investigation, 2nd Edition, Charles River Media, 2005
Cyber Law Simplified, Vivek Sood, Pub: TMH
Cyber Security by Nina Godbole, Sunit Belapure, Pub: Wiley‑India
Information Warfare: Corporate attack and defense in digital world, William Hutchinson, Mathew Warren, Elsevier
Cyber Laws and IT Protection, Harish Chander, Pub: PHI

modules.title: Introduction to computer networks and OSI model
topics: Fundamentals of computer networks and their uses
ISO‑OSI layered architecture and peer‑to‑peer processes
Encapsulation and functions/services of OSI layers
Physical layer: digital signals, transmission impairments, maximum data rate
Circuit, packet and message switching, virtual circuit concept
Introduction to ISDN and its components

modules.title: Data link layer protocols and medium access
topics: Design issues and functions of the data link layer
Framing methods – fixed‑size, variable‑size, bit stuffing, byte stuffing
Data link protocols – Stop‑and‑Wait, Sliding window, PPP, SLIP, HDLC
Medium access sublayer – static and dynamic channel allocation

modules.title: LAN standards, devices and network layer basics
topics: LAN devices – hubs, switches (learning, cut‑through, store‑and‑forward)
Network layer design issues and functions

modules.title: Routing algorithms and TCP/IP fundamentals
topics: Routing algorithms – shortest‑path, flooding, link‑state (LSR), distance‑vector, hierarchical routing
IP datagrams with options and delivery process

modules.title: Subnetting, transport and application layer services
topics: Transport‑layer congestion control, load shedding and jitter control
Connection establishment, release and flow control
Application‑layer services – DNS and electronic mail

reference_books: “Computer Networks” – Tanenbaum, PHI Learning
“Data Communication & Networks”, Forouzan, TMH
“TCP/IP – Protocol Suite”, Forouzan, TMH, 3rd edition
“Computer Networks and Internets”, D. E. Comer, Pearson
“TCP/IP Illustrated”, W. Richard Stevens, Volume I, Addison‑Wesley
“Internetworking with TCP/IP”, Vol. I‑III, Comer, PHI Learning

modules.title: Introduction to Operating Systems
topics: Function and evolution of operating systems
Desirable characteristics and features of an OS

modules.title: Process Management and Inter‑Process Communication
topics: CPU scheduling algorithms and deterministic modeling
Process management in UNIX and Windows
Deadlock problems, characterization, prevention, avoidance, recovery
Inter‑process communication in UNIX and Windows

modules.title: Memory Management
topics: Partitioning, swapping, segmentation, paging, and paged segmentation
Virtual memory: concept and demand‑paging implementation
Memory management in UNIX and Windows

modules.title: File Systems Management
topics: File concept and user vs system‑programmer view
Disk‑space allocation methods: contiguous, linked, indexed
File systems in UNIX and Windows

modules.title: Input/Output Management
topics: I/O problems and types of I/O operations (program‑controlled, interrupt‑driven, concurrent, asynchronous)
Introduction to network, distributed and multiprocessor operating systems
I/O management in UNIX and Windows

reference_books: Andrew S. Tanenbaum, “Modern Operating Systems”, 3rd edition, Prentice Hall
Maurice J. Bach, “The Design of Unix Operating System”, Prentice Hall of India
Bovet & Cesati, “Understanding the Linux Kernel”, O'Reilly, 2nd edition

modules.title: Basic Concepts of Data and DBMS
topics: Basic concepts of data and DBMS
Introduction to DBMS and differences with traditional file storage systems

modules.title: Relational Data Model and SQL
topics: Relational data model: domains, tuples, attributes, relations
Relational algebra operations: select, project, rename, join, union, intersection, difference, division, product
SQL constraints, joins, and nested queries

modules.title: Normalization Theory and Database Methodologies
topics: Normalization theory and database design methodologies
Axioms and inference rules for functional dependencies

modules.title: Transaction Processing and Concurrency Control
topics: Transaction processing concepts and transaction states
Types of serializability and testing methods
Concurrency control mechanisms: two‑phase locking, timestamp‑based control
Deadlock management: avoidance, prevention, detection, resolution
Recovery techniques: deferred update, immediate update, shadow paging, checkpoints

modules.title: Storage Structures and Query Optimization
topics: Storage structures and secondary storage devices
Hashing and indexing structures: single‑level and multi‑level indices
Query processing and optimization algorithms for select, project, join
Case study of a contemporary DBMS

reference_books: Korth, Silbertz, Sudarshan, “Database Concepts”, McGraw Hill.
Elmasri, Navathe, “Fundamentals of Database Systems”, Pearson.
Ivan Bayross, “SQL, PL/SQL the Programming Language of Oracle”, BPB publications.
S. Sharma, J. Agrawal, S. Agrawal, “Advanced Database Management System”, Dreamtech Press.
Leon & Leon, “Fundamental of Data Base Management System”, TMH
subjects.extra: Course Objective: The objective of this course is to enable students in developing a high level understanding of the concepts of Database management systems in contrast with traditional data management systems with emphasis on skills to apply these concepts in building, maintaining and retrieving data from these DBMS.

modules.title: Socket Programming
topics: Client-side programming using python: TCP and UDP based clients and servers
High-level library modules for standard Internet and web-related services (HTTP, FTP, XML-RPC, etc.)
urllib2 module for interacting with web servers

modules.title: Internet Data Handling
topics: Processing common Internet data formats such as HTML, XML, and JSON

modules.title: Web Programming
topics: Basics of web programming in Python

modules.title: Thread Programming
topics: Overview of concurrent programming with threads and advanced Python programming idioms
Python interpreter execution model and the GIL

modules.title: Message Passing and Data Serialization
topics: Interprocess communication, message passing, and data serialization
Multiprocessing: process objects, pipes, queues, shared memory objects, and process pools

modules.title: Distributed Computing
topics: Core programming concepts such as actors, client/server computing, REST, remote procedure call, and distributed objects
Use of multiprocessing library to implement distributed objects

modules.title: Generators and Coroutines
topics: Using generators and coroutines to implement cooperative multitasking

reference_books: Martin C. Brown, “Python: The Complete Reference”, McGraw-Hill publication, 2001
SakisKasampalisQuan Nguyen Dr Gabriele Lanaro Dr. Gabriele Lanaro, “Advanced Python Programming: Build high performance, concurrent, and multi-threaded apps with Python”, Packt publication, 2019
Micha Gorelick & lanOzsvaid, “High Performance Python”, O’Reilly publication,2020
